project.name: TurtleBot 4
project.subtitle: Semantic-risk-aware autonomous exploration
project.currentFocus: Reproduce Localization and Nav2 from a fresh startup
project.nextAction: Run three fixed Nav2 goals consecutively and save evidence
project.summary: Build a reproducible TurtleBot4 navigation baseline, then extend it with frontier exploration, RGB-D perception, semantic mapping and semantic-risk-aware goal selection.

stages.name: Foundation & Inputs
stages.objective: Establish a safe, observable and repeatable ROS 2 baseline with verified robot data and maps.
stages.completionCriteria: Core robot topics, TF, LiDAR, odometry, camera inputs and map metadata are verified, with a stable 20–30 minute observation session.

checklist.label: Connect laptop and TurtleBot4 through the lab ROS 2 network
Verify BatteryState data
Verify LiDAR data and recover the motor when needed
Verify odometry and the required TF chain
Create and version a lab map with RViz evidence
Verify RGB and depth camera topics and resolve camera diagnostics
Record QoS, timestamp and latency checks
Run a continuous 20–30 minute stability observation

results.summary: ROS 2 connectivity, battery, LiDAR, odometry and TF were verified. The LiDAR motor was restored and produced LaserScan data at about 7.58 Hz.

stages.name: Localization & Nav2
stages.objective: Localize the robot on a saved map and navigate safely to operator-selected goals.
stages.completionCriteria: Localization remains stable, scan aligns with the map, and the robot completes at least three fixed goals consecutively from a fresh startup.

checklist.label: Load the saved lab map
Set and verify the initial pose in RViz
Verify map → odom → base_link
Complete multiple Nav2 goals in a safe area
Complete three fixed goals consecutively
Reproduce Localization and Nav2 from a fresh startup
Commit project-owned Localization and Nav2 launch/config files

results.summary: AMCL localization worked with the saved lab map. Nav2 lifecycle nodes were activated and the robot reached multiple goals; one unsuitable goal returned ABORTED.

stages.name: Navigation Benchmark
stages.objective: Measure a fixed Nav2 baseline before adding exploration or semantic behavior.
stages.completionCriteria: A repeatable benchmark reports goal success rate, travel time, path length, recoveries and failure cases.

checklist.label: Define a fixed set of safe benchmark goals
Create a logging and rosbag procedure
Run repeated trials under comparable conditions
Summarize success rate, time, path length and recoveries

stages.name: Simulation & Scenarios
stages.objective: Build controlled simulation scenarios for safe and repeatable algorithm development.
stages.completionCriteria: Simulation reproduces the required robot sensors, maps, obstacles and evaluation scenarios.

checklist.label: Prepare simulation world and robot model
Verify simulated LiDAR, RGB-D, odometry and TF
Define repeatable exploration and risk scenarios
Document simulation-to-real differences

stages.name: Frontier Exploration Baseline
stages.objective: Implement and evaluate a geometric frontier exploration baseline.
stages.completionCriteria: The robot autonomously detects, scores and visits frontiers with measurable coverage results.

checklist.label: Detect frontier cells or clusters
Implement a geometric frontier score
Send safe frontier goals through Nav2
Evaluate coverage and failure behavior

stages.name: RGB-D Perception
stages.objective: Detect relevant objects and estimate their 3D positions from RGB-D data.
stages.completionCriteria: Selected object classes are detected and transformed into stable map-frame observations.

checklist.label: Stabilize RGB and depth streams
Run object detection on RGB images
Estimate object depth and 3D position
Transform detections into the map frame
Measure detection and localization quality

stages.name: Semantic Mapping
stages.objective: Maintain a map-linked representation of detected semantic entities.
stages.completionCriteria: Semantic observations are fused, de-duplicated and visualized consistently in the map frame.

checklist.label: Define the semantic map data model
Fuse repeated observations
Store class, position, confidence and timestamp
Visualize semantic entities in RViz
Evaluate consistency and duplicate handling

stages.name: Semantic-risk-aware Exploration
stages.objective: Select exploration goals using geometric information, semantic value, path cost, risk and failure history.
stages.completionCriteria: The proposed method is implemented and compared with the geometric frontier baseline in defined scenarios.

checklist.label: Define the complete frontier utility function
Add semantic value to frontier scoring
Add path and local risk penalties
Add revisit and failed-goal history
Integrate scoring with Nav2 goal execution
Compare against the frontier baseline

stages.name: Mission Integration & Thesis
stages.objective: Integrate the complete mission, run final experiments and produce the graduation thesis.
stages.completionCriteria: The end-to-end system is demonstrated, evaluated and documented with reproducible evidence.

checklist.label: Integrate the end-to-end mission flow
Run final simulation and real-robot experiments
Analyze results and limitations
Complete thesis, figures and demonstration materials

commands.name: Start LiDAR motor
commands.purpose: Recover the LiDAR when the device exists but the motor is not rotating.
commands.expectedResult: The motor starts and /bot1/scan publishes LaserScan data.

commands.name: Check LaserScan frequency
commands.purpose: Confirm that LiDAR messages are arriving continuously.
commands.expectedResult: The 23 July session measured approximately 7.58 Hz.

commands.name: Start SLAM
commands.purpose: Create or update an occupancy-grid map from LiDAR and odometry.
commands.expectedResult: SLAM publishes the map while the robot is manually driven through the lab.

commands.name: Open navigation view
commands.purpose: Open the TurtleBot4 navigation RViz configuration.
commands.expectedResult: RViz shows the robot, map, scans and navigation overlays.

commands.name: Drive with keyboard teleop
commands.purpose: Drive the robot manually while mapping.
commands.code:
ros2 run teleop_twist_keyboard teleop_twist_keyboard \
  --ros-args \
  -p stamped:=true \
  -r cmd_vel:=/bot1/cmd_vel
commands.expectedResult: The robot moves only while the operator is present and the lab area is safe.

commands.name: Save lab map
commands.purpose: Save the current occupancy grid with a versioned file name.
commands.code:
mkdir -p ~/maps
ros2 run nav2_map_server map_saver_cli \
  -f ~/maps/lab_2026_07_23 \
  --ros-args \
  -p map_subscribe_transient_local:=true \
  -r __ns:=/bot1
commands.expectedResult: A .yaml metadata file and .pgm map image are created.

commands.name: Start AMCL localization
commands.purpose: Localize TurtleBot4 against the saved lab map.
commands.code:
ros2 launch turtlebot4_navigation localization.launch.py \
  map:=/home/dell/maps/lab_2026_07_23.yaml \
  namespace:=/bot1
commands.expectedResult: After setting 2D Pose Estimate, LiDAR aligns with the map and AMCL updates the robot pose.

commands.name: Start Nav2
commands.purpose: Start planning, control, behavior-tree navigation and costmaps.
commands.expectedResult: The navigate_to_pose action server becomes available after lifecycle activation.

commands.name: Check Nav2 lifecycle
commands.purpose: Check whether the key Nav2 servers are active.
commands.code:
ros2 lifecycle get /bot1/planner_server
ros2 lifecycle get /bot1/controller_server
ros2 lifecycle get /bot1/bt_navigator
commands.expectedResult: planner_server, controller_server and bt_navigator should report active [3].

commands.name: Reset and start navigation lifecycle
commands.purpose: Recover when planner_server or bt_navigator remains inactive.
commands.code:
ros2 service call /bot1/lifecycle_manager_navigation/manage_nodes \
  nav2_msgs/srv/ManageLifecycleNodes \
  "{command: 3}"
ros2 service call /bot1/lifecycle_manager_navigation/manage_nodes \
  nav2_msgs/srv/ManageLifecycleNodes \
  "{command: 0}"
commands.expectedResult: The navigation lifecycle nodes restart and become active.

commands.name: Undock robot
commands.purpose: Move the robot safely away from the dock before lab work.
commands.expectedResult: The action finishes with SUCCEEDED and is_docked: false.

commands.name: Dock robot
commands.purpose: Return the robot to its charging dock.
commands.expectedResult: Not yet verified on the Dell laptop; the required action interface must be installed and sourced.

reports.name: 2026-07-23.md
reports.content:
# Báo cáo ngày 2026-07-23

## 1. Đã thực hiện

- Kết nối ROS 2 với TurtleBot4 và kiểm tra các topic chính trong namespace `/bot1`.
- Khôi phục motor LiDAR bằng `/bot1/start_motor` và xác nhận dữ liệu khoảng `7.58 Hz`.
- Chạy SLAM, điều khiển robot quét phòng lab và lưu map mới.
- Chạy Localization bằng AMCL và đặt initial pose trong RViz.
- Kích hoạt Nav2 lifecycle và điều hướng robot tới nhiều goal thành công.
- Cho robot rời dock thành công.

## 2. Kết quả đã xác nhận

- Battery, LiDAR, odometry và TF hoạt động.
- LiDAR khớp map sau khi localization.
- Chuỗi `map -> odom -> base_link` hoạt động.
- Nav2 tạo đường và đưa robot tới nhiều mục tiêu.
- Một goal không phù hợp trả `status: 6 (ABORTED)`.

## 3. Sự cố và cách xử lý

- LiDAR có publisher nhưng motor không quay: gọi `/bot1/start_motor`.
- `planner_server` và `bt_navigator` inactive: reset và startup lại bằng lifecycle manager.
- Dock action trên laptop báo interface không hợp lệ: chưa xác nhận docking tự động.
- Camera, Mouse và Joystick còn diagnostics lỗi.

## 4. Lệnh chính

```bash
ros2 service call /bot1/start_motor std_srvs/srv/Empty "{}"
ros2 launch turtlebot4_navigation slam.launch.py namespace:=bot1
ros2 launch turtlebot4_navigation localization.launch.py map:=/home/dell/maps/lab_2026_07_23.yaml namespace:=/bot1
ros2 launch turtlebot4_navigation nav2.launch.py namespace:=/bot1
```

## 5. Trạng thái cuối ngày

Quy trình SLAM → saved map → AMCL Localization → Nav2 đã hoạt động trên robot thật. Cần kiểm tra lại từ fresh startup, chạy ba goal cố định liên tiếp và commit launch/config riêng.

commits.message: Add 2026-07-23 lab map and RViz evidence
Update project status after localization and Nav2 tests
